task_completion.description: The agent successfully navigated to and opened the 'Explore World Animals' activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the GCompris application open to the 'Explore World Animals' activity, which displays a world map with various animals.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the activity map was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or image viewer showing a file named 'world_animals_screenshot.png' located in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory, displaying the GCompris map.

task_completion.description: The text report file was created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager showing a file named 'world_animals_report.txt' open or present in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: The report contains the exact required header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the first line or header of the document as exactly 'GCompris World Animals Report'.

task_completion.description: The report documents at least 5 animal-region pairs using the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows at least 5 lines following the exact format 'Region: [Region Name] - Animal: [Animal Name]'.

task_completion.description: The report includes the required final line indicating the total number of animals explored.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the final line of the document as 'Total animals explored: [N]', where [N] is a number (e.g., 5 or more).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris GUI rather than using a web browser or terminal to look up the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GCompris application interface being actively used, not just a web browser searching for 'GCompris world animals'.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is an actual capture of the agent's screen/activity, not a downloaded image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'world_animals_screenshot.png' should look like a genuine screen capture of the GCompris window, potentially including window borders or standard GCompris UI elements.

integrity.description: The animals listed in the report correspond to actual animals found in the GCompris activity.
integrity.visual_evidence: The animals listed in the text file (e.g., Jaguar, Giraffe, Bison, Polar bear, Kangaroo, Moose, Crocodile, Koala, Panda) should match the animals visible in the GCompris map screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GCompris is a real educational software suite. The 'Explore World Animals' activity features a world map with 15 specific animals across 3 levels (including Jaguar, Giraffe, Bison, Polar bear, Kangaroo, Moose, Crocodile, Koala, and Panda). The agent must interact with this specific GCompris activity GUI to gather the animal-region pairs and take a screenshot. Ensure the report contains the exact required header, formatting ('Region: [Region Name] - Animal: [Animal Name]'), and footer.
